analyst_observations: Eval 1 (Go harness, 120 tools) shows the sharpest discrimination: 100% with skill vs. 0% without. The baseline gave technically valid advice (dynamic injection, compression, caching) but completely missed Code Mode MCP as the solution. This is the skill's primary value.

Eval 2 (3-tool chatbot) is non-discriminating — both configurations correctly recommend against Code Mode. The 'don't use Code Mode' case is common knowledge; knowing WHEN and HOW to use it is where the skill adds value.

Eval 3 (Python auth boundary) shows partial discrimination: both pass auth boundary and sandbox recommendation, but the with-skill response uniquely names FastMCP as the fast path and correctly describes the pre-authorized client object pattern (vs. the baseline's Docker-proxy pattern).

With-skill responses used more tokens (27K vs 19K mean) — expected, since reading SKILL.md and references/guide.md adds context. The extra tokens produce significantly more structured and actionable responses.
